next_steps: Induced sputum for PJP and AFB staining
Bronchoscopy with bronchoalveolar lavage for cytology, microbiology, and PCR testing
Serum Histoplasma antigen and Beta-D-glucan testing
Blood cultures for bacteria, fungi, and mycobacteria
CT chest with intravenous contrast to characterize pulmonary nodules and hilar adenopathy
Initiation of empiric treatment for PJP and severe sepsis
Initiate empiric treatment for PJP with high-dose trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole and adjunctive corticosteroids
Perform bronchoscopy with bronchoalveolar lavage for PJP PCR, AFB smear/culture, fungal stains/culture, and cytology
Obtain serum and urine Histoplasma antigen testing
Obtain high-resolution CT chest to assess for nodal involvement and parenchymal architecture
Obtain blood cultures for mycobacteria, fungi, and bacteria
Assess for adrenal insufficiency given hypotension and disseminated fungal/mycobacterial risk
Consult Infectious Disease and Pulmonology services
